segments.text: Then Job replied:
Listen carefully to my words; let this be the consolation you give me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Bear with me while I speak, and after I have spoken, mock on.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Is my complaint directed to a human being? Why should I not be impatient?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Look at me and be appalled; clap your hand over your mouth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I think about this, I am terrified; trembling seizes my body.
Why do the wicked live on, growing old and increasing in power?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They see their children established around them, their offspring before their eyes.
Their homes are safe and free from fear; the rod of God is not on them.
Their bulls never fail to breed; their cows calve and do not miscarry.
They send forth their children as a flock; their little ones dance about.
They sing to the music of timbrel and lyre; they make merry to the sound of the pipe.
They spend their years in prosperity and go down to the grave in peace.
Leave us alone! We have no desire to know your ways.
segments.speaker: The Wicked

segments.text: Who is the Almighty, that we should serve him? What would we gain by praying to him?
segments.speaker: The Wicked

segments.text: But their prosperity is not in their own hands, so I stand aloof from the plans of the wicked.
Yet how often is the lamp of the wicked snuffed out? How often does calamity come upon them, the fate God allots in his anger?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How often are they like straw before the wind, like chaff swept away by a gale?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: It is said, 'God stores up the punishment of the wicked for their children.' Let him repay the wicked, so that they themselves will experience it!
Let their own eyes see their destruction; let them drink the cup of the wrath of the Almighty.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For what do they care about the families they leave behind when their allotted months come to an end?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Can anyone teach knowledge to God, since he judges even the highest?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: One person dies in full vigor, completely secure and at ease,
well nourished in body, bones rich with marrow.
Another dies in bitterness of soul, never having enjoyed anything good.
Side by side they lie in the dust, and worms cover them both.
I know full well what you are thinking, the schemes by which you would wrong me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Where now is the house of the great, the tents where the wicked lived?
segments.speaker: Job's Friends

segments.text: Have you never questioned those who travel? Have you paid no regard to their accounts—
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: that the wicked are spared from the day of calamity, that they are delivered from the day of wrath?
Who denounces their conduct to their face? Who repays them for what they have done?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They are carried to the grave, and watch is kept over their tombs.
The soil in the valley is sweet to them; everyone follows after them, and a countless throng goes before them.
So how can you console me with your nonsense? Nothing is left of your answers but falsehood!
segments.speaker: Job
